entries.title: Continents of the World
entries.content: Seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania (Australia), South America. Asia is largest by area and population.

entries.title: Longest Rivers
entries.content: Nile (~6,650 km) and Amazon (~6,400 km) compete for longest. Amazon carries greatest water volume. Ganges, Yangtze, and Mississippi are also major.

entries.title: Tectonic Plates
entries.content: Earth's lithosphere is divided into plates that move slowly. Collisions form mountains; separation forms rifts. Earthquakes and volcanoes occur at boundaries.

entries.title: Climate Zones
entries.content: Tropical (near equator), dry (deserts), temperate (moderate seasons), continental (large seasonal swings), polar (cold year-round).

entries.title: Mount Everest
entries.content: Highest mountain above sea level at 8,849 m (29,032 ft) in the Himalayas on the Nepal–China border. Formed by Indian and Eurasian plate collision.
